01 — The problem

We have a connection problem

We're more connected than ever. Yet meaningful conversation is becoming rare. Networking feels transactional. Social media feels performative. Most people are surrounded by content, and starved of connection.

02 — The idea

The best conversations happen side by side

Not across a boardroom table.
Not at a networking event.
Not through a screen.

Something changes when people walk.

01

Walk

Move your body.

02

Talk

Share ideas.

03

Grow

Leave better than you arrived.

04 — Who it's for

Founders. Creators. Builders. People who are actively making something.

05 — The format

What happens on a walk

01 — Meet

Meet

Arrive solo. Find your group on the riverside, coffee in hand.

02 — Walk

Walk

An hour through London, on foot, at a pace built for talking.

03 — Talk

Talk

Conversation, unscripted. No pitch decks. No name tags.

04 — Connect

Connect

Numbers exchanged where it feels right. Nothing forced.

06 — Why people come

Not testimonials. Just people.

07 — The science

Creativity

Mental wellbeing

Stress reduction

Better conversations

Stronger relationships

Walking doesn't just change how we feel. It changes how we think, connect and perform.

Something measurable happens to a conversation once you stop facing each other and start facing the same direction.

Research from Harvard Health, Mayo Clinic and peer-reviewed studies consistently links walking with improvements in mood, cognition, wellbeing and social connection.

08 — The comparison

A different kind of network

09 — The vision

One community.
Many cities.

London Miami New York Dubai Singapore

London today. More tomorrow.
